entities.name: SOYABEAN
entities.description: The legume crop used in the hydroponic study to test the effects of CO2 and ureide on nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: CARBON DIOXIDE
entities.description: The atmospheric gas provided at ambient (360 µmol mol-1) or elevated (700 µmol mol-1) levels.

entities.name: UREIDE
entities.description: A nitrogen-rich compound added to the nutrient solution that inhibits N2-fixation.

entities.name: N2-FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia, measured via nitrogenase activity.

entities.name: ACETYLENE REDUCTION ASSAY
entities.description: A technique used to measure the activity of nitrogenase and thus the rate of N2-fixation.

entities.name: TOTAL NONSTRUCTURAL CARBOHYDRATE
entities.description: The sum of soluble sugars and starch in plant tissues, which correlates negatively with ureide levels.

entities.name: UREIDE CATABOLISM
entities.description: The metabolic breakdown of ureides, whose products are suggested to regulate N2-fixation.

entities.name: NODULE NITROGENASE ACTIVITY
entities.description: The enzymatic activity within root nodules responsible for N2-fixation.

relationships.description: Elevated levels alleviate the inhibition of N2-fixation caused by ureide
Inhibits N2-fixation activity in soyabean plants
Used as a method to measure the rate of N2-fixation
The crop species in which N2-fixation is occurring
Induces a significant decrease in TNC concentrations in leaves and nodules
Elevated levels result in consistently higher TNC concentrations
The products of this process are critical in the regulation of N2-fixation
The specific enzymatic activity that drives the process of N2-fixation
Causes a decline in the activity of nitrogenase in nodules
